Skip to contents
CoSparTA 0.1.0
  • Reference
    • CoSparTA Pipeline Walkthrough

Retrieve Factor Matrices or Weights from a CoSparTA Fit

get_loadings.Rd

Unified getter that retrieves a named output from a fitted CoSparTA object by logical name, so users do not need to navigate the internal slot structure (e.g.\ fit$res$ql$El_normed).

Usage

get_loadings(fit, what = "U1", normalized = TRUE)

Arguments

fit

A fitted object returned by CoSparTA or CoSparTA_missing.

what

Character string specifying what to retrieve. One of:

"U1"

Observation loading matrix (n x K).

"U2"

Time factor matrix (p x K).

"U3"

Channel weight matrix (w x K).

"weight"

Component weight vector \(\lambda\) of length K (product of mode norms; sorted descending when normalized = TRUE).

"gamma"

List of length K of covariate coefficient vectors (one per component). NULL entries indicate unsupervised components.

normalized

Logical. If TRUE (default), returns the normalized and reordered version stored in the _normed fields (unit-norm columns, descending \(\lambda\) order). If FALSE, returns the raw posterior estimates in original component order.

Value

The requested object:

"U1", "U2", "U3"

A numeric matrix.

"weight"

A numeric vector of length K.

"gamma"

A list of length K.

See also

CoSparTA, normalize_factors

Examples

if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{
fit <- CoSparTA(X, K = 3, Xcov = Xcov)

# Normalized observation loadings (n x 3, unit-norm columns)
U1 <- get_loadings(fit, "U1")

# Raw observation loadings
U1_raw <- get_loadings(fit, "U1", normalized = FALSE)

# Component weights (descending)
w <- get_loadings(fit, "weight")

# Covariate coefficient lists
gamma <- get_loadings(fit, "gamma")
gamma[[1]]  # gamma for component 1
} # }
